episode0.title: THE DEAD ENGINEER — DIRECTOR'S CUT V2

puzzle0.title: DEATH RECORD CORRELATION
puzzle0.description: Match the recovered records with the correct contradiction.

event1.text: Before we go anywhere near it, I need you to understand something.

event0.text: Curiosity is how this station got most of its scars.

event1.text: Scientists call it discovery. Engineers call it troubleshooting. Management calls it initiative.

event2.text: The rest of us called it Tuesday.

event3.text: Also sleep deprivation. Never underestimate what three months of bad sleep can do to a man's sense of theatre.

event2.text: Some signals should stay buried. Yes, I know how dramatic that sounds.

event3.text: Unfortunately, space has made me very dramatic.

event0.text: You are going straight for the thing I asked you not to touch.
I respect the consistency. I hate the survival instinct.

event1.text: 7.14 was a backup emergency carrier. Quiet frequency, low traffic, ugly signal profile.

event2.text: The sort of channel you build because regulations require redundancy, then hope you never need.

event1.text: Fine. But if that channel answers us, remember this wasn't curiosity. It was a door handle.
I hate that the unknown voice gave useful advice. That feels unfair.

event0.text: For the record, if a haunted signal starts giving better procedural guidance than I do, I am filing a formal complaint.

event1.title: PERSONNEL FILE: ETHAN HALE
event1.content:
DR. ETHAN HALE
ROLE: CHIEF ENGINEER
ASSIGNMENT: ORBITAL RESEARCH STATION ECHO
STATUS: DECEASED
TIME OF DEATH: 21:20
LOCATION: DOOR 12 ACCESS CORRIDOR
event1.caption: Recovered personnel record from corrupted station cache.

event2.text: No. Sorry. Don't read it again. I read it. I just needed the universe to make a different choice the second time.

event4.text: I've been called a lot of things.

event5.text: Chief Engineer. Workaholic. Condescending, once, by a man who used the word 'synergy' unironically.

event7.text: Do I sound dead to you?

event8.text: That was not rhetorical. I am genuinely asking because my confidence in basic categories is collapsing.

event4.text: Or the system is wrong. Please let it be the system.

event1.text: I am trying very hard not to think about what that means while still continuing to breathe.

event0.text: Which, by the way, is an extremely inconvenient thing to do while looking at your own death certificate.

event7.text: There should be a manual for this. 'So You've Been Officially Dead for Months.'

event1.text: I need it to be. That is not science. That is terror wearing a lab coat.

event5.text: Records corrupt all the time. Timestamps drift. Personnel caches duplicate entries. Once the mess hall system listed me as a vegetarian for six weeks.

event10.text: But this doesn't look like mess hall corruption.

event0.text: I am here. I am speaking to you.

event3.text: I really wish it had said something less horrible.

event5.text: I am tired. I am scared. I am extremely irritated by the archive UI.

event18.text: Also less accurate. It is developing a talent for being upsetting in very specific ways.

choice0.text: WHAT DO YOU REMEMBER ABOUT DOOR 12?

choice1.text: TALK TO ME. WHAT DO YOU MISS?

event5.text: I know pieces. I just don't know if they're mine.

event8.text: I remember the door. The handle. The warning light above it.

event10.text: I remember the seal making this soft little click. Ridiculous thing to remember, isn't it?

event12.text: Not the death. Not the person. The click.

event15.text: There was shouting. Someone said my name like I had already done something unforgivable.

event17.text: And then... a pressure drop. Or the memory of one.

event21.text: Like somebody cut a page out of my life with a knife.

event2.text: [HALE RESPONSE PARTIALLY WITHHELD: EMOTIONAL STRESS DETECTED]

event3.text: Avoiding the obvious horror with paperwork. That's very station of us.

event1.text: But I need to know. If that file is real, then something is using my signal.

event2.text: And I don't like sharing. Especially with ghosts.

event9.text: For the record, I was bad at sharing before the ghosts.

event1.title: AUTOPSY FRAGMENT: HALE
event1.content:
SUBJECT: ETHAN HALE
CAUSE OF DEATH: DECOMPRESSION TRAUMA
LOCATION: DOOR 12 ACCESS CORRIDOR
RECOVERY STATUS: BODY NOT RECOVERED
NOTE: VITALS TERMINATED AT 21:20
ARCHIVE INTEGRITY: 68%
event1.caption: Recovered emergency medical fragment.

event8.text: If they found a body, at least the nightmare has a shape.

event10.text: A place. A bag. A report nobody wants to sign.

event12.text: But not recovered means the station either lost me...

event14.text: ...or it found something it refused to call a body.

event17.text: Listen to me. I sound like a man arguing with a filing cabinet.

event19.text: This is exactly why I hated medical logs. They make tragedy sound organized.

event1.title: VOICE SIGNATURE REPORT
event1.content:
ACTIVE SPEAKER: ETHAN HALE
VOICEPRINT MATCH: 84%
SIGNAL DELAY: NONLINEAR
CARRIER TRACE: 7.14 MHz harmonic residue detected
event1.caption: Recovered voiceprint comparison from live relay traffic.
event1.message: [VOICE SIGNATURE REPORT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event5.text: That is just high enough to ruin my day and low enough to avoid answering anything.

event7.text: Nonlinear delay. 7.14 residue. Wonderful. Even my voice is doing suspicious things now.

event1.title: TIMELINE CONFLICT
event1.content:
21:14 — Door 12 opened.
21:17 — Security override accepted.
21:20 — Ethan Hale declared deceased.

Conflict: Active communication signature matches Ethan Hale after recorded time of death.
Secondary conflict: Communication signature contains 7.14 MHz harmonic residue.
event1.caption: Automated timeline conflict report.

event5.text: A neat little contradiction with timestamps and labels.

event7.text: The system says I died at 21:20. The relay says I kept talking after.

event9.text: And somewhere in between those two statements, I am supposed to exist.

event1.text: No. Those records don't line up that way.

event4.text: Try again, but don't force the evidence to comfort us. It will not be kind enough for that.

event3.text: So that's the shape of it.

event5.text: A dead engineer with no body. A living signal with my voice. A channel that keeps leaving fingerprints.

event8.text: I would like to formally resign from being a mystery.

event0.text: I can't explain it because I don't know what I am explaining.

event1.text: And every time you say my name like an accusation, it gets harder to think.

event5.text: But I am trying. I need you to understand that. I am not dodging this because it hurts.

event7.text: I am dodging it because every answer seems to bite back.

event8.text: I wish I hadn't, but I did.

event1.text: There was another voice on the emergency channel.

event5.text: Not the unknown one. Not exactly.

event7.text: It sounded... close. Like hearing someone through a wall and realizing they know your name.

event13.text: Operator... I don't think we're opening a frequency.

event15.text: I think we're about to meet somebody.

event2.text: No— I said stabilize it first.

event3.text: The channel is open now. We did not lose anyone. But we lost control of how it opens.

event8.text: There is a difference between unlocking a door and having something pull it from the other side.

event6.text: I never thought I would say that. I have mocked slow systems professionally.

event8.text: But right now slow feels like a handrail.

event14.text: Hale is silent for a moment.

event3.text: That is probably the least scientific comfort available, and right now I am choosing to treasure it.

event5.text: I can hear the relay hum. I can feel the deck vibrating under my boots.

event7.text: I am annoyed by you, which is historically a living-person activity.

event9.text: So let's keep that theory alive for as long as we can.

event3.text: Not because the answer is tragic.

event5.text: Because the first thing that came to mind was a coffee machine.

event8.text: Not even good coffee. Station coffee. Industrial regret in a cup.

event10.text: Every morning the machine made a noise like a small animal giving up on life.

event12.text: Every morning I said I would fix it properly.

event14.text: Every morning I hit it with the heel of my hand and called that engineering.

event17.text: I miss that. Normal broken things.

event19.text: A pump. A bad hinge. Someone complaining that gravity simulation made their knees feel weird.

event21.text: Problems that didn't ask whether I was dead.

event3.text: If I talk, I don't have to picture a corridor with my name on a death report.

event6.text: You know what I don't miss? Station management.

event8.text: I spent three months warning them the relay spine was unstable.

event12.text: Do you know what I got?

event17.text: Never trust anyone who schedules a meeting to discuss a warning alarm.

event19.text: If an alarm is warning you, it has already completed the meeting.

event3.text: Personnel file says I was declared dead at 21:20.

event5.text: Autopsy fragment says decompression trauma, but no body recovered.

event7.text: Timeline conflict says my signal kept talking after my recorded death.

event9.text: I hate that sentence. I hate that it is useful.
